commonIssues:
In Tamil Nadu, the face-authentication for home-based LL tests is very strict. Applications are often rejected if the photo on Aadhaar is very old (e.g., from childhood).

How to fix: If face-authentication fails twice, update your Aadhaar photo at a post office or Aadhaar center. Alternatively, visit the RTO in person to take the test on their computer, which uses manual ID verification.

RTOs like Chennai (West) or Coimbatore often have a backlog of 3–4 months for printing physical smart cards.

How to fix: Check your status on the 'Application Status' section of Parivahan. If it shows 'DL Printed', it will be sent by speed post. Meanwhile, the digital DL in DigiLocker is legally valid for all checks in Tamil Nadu.

Applicants sometimes face issues during the physical test if they do not wear proper safety gear (ISI-marked helmet for 2-wheelers, seatbelt for 4-wheelers) or fail to follow hand signals.

How to fix: Always wear an ISI-certified helmet and use seatbelts during the test. Practice hand signals (slowing down, turning, stopping) as examiners in TN still emphasize these alongside electronic indicators.

Confusion over renewal frequency and medical requirements once a driver reaches age 50.

How to fix: After age 50, a DL is renewed every 5 years (instead of 10 or 20). You must submit a Medical Certificate (Form 1A) signed by a government-registered doctor via the Sarathi portal for each renewal.
